fundamentals.name: Deere & Company
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Deere & Company engages in the manufacture and distribution of various equipment worldwide. The company operates through four segments: Production and Precision Agriculture, Small Agriculture and Turf, Construction and Forestry, and Financial Services. The Production and Precision Agriculture segment provides four-wheel-drive track and row crop tractors, harvesters, cotton pickers and strippers, sugarcane harvesters and loaders, soil preparation, tillage, seeding, and crop care equipment, as well as application equipment, including sprayers and nutrient management, soil preparation machinery, and related attachments and service parts. The Small Agriculture and Turf segment offers specialty, utility, and compact tractors; self-propelled forage harvesters and attachments; rotary mowers, hay and forage equipment, and utility vehicles; turf and utility equipment, including riding lawn, commercial mowing, and golf course equipment, and utility vehicles, as well as implements for mowing, tilling, snow and debris handling, aerating, residential, commercial, golf, and sports turf care applications. The Construction and Forestry segment provides backhoe loaders, crawler dozers and loaders, four-wheel-drive loaders, excavators, motor graders, articulated dump trucks, skid-steer loaders, milling machines, recyclers, slipform and asphalt pavers, surface miners, compactors, tandem, static rollers, mobile crushers and screens, mobile and stationary asphalt plants, and log harvesters; and road building and rehabilitation equipment. The Financial Services segment finances sales and leases agriculture and turf, and construction and forestry equipment. It also offers wholesale financing to dealers of the foregoing equipment; and extended equipment warranties. Deere & Company has a strategic partnership with Tarter USA to develop and produce flex wing rotary cutters. The company was founded in 1837 and is headquartered in Moline, Illinois.

geographic.name: Asia Africa Oceania And Middle East
Central Europe And Commonwealth Of Independent States

judgment.summary: A company with strong margins and returns, but facing revenue contraction and high debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of 6.55 vs 5.74; next earnings date is 2026-08-20.
positives: Profit margin 10.1% and operating margin 17.5% are robust.
Return on equity at 18.3% indicates efficient use of shareholder equity.
Forward P/E of 23.51 is lower than trailing P/E of 30.48, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $644.63.
Last quarter's EPS beat estimates by 14.1%.
negatives: Revenue growth is negative at -11.1% YoY.
Earnings growth is negative at -8.5% YoY.
Debt/Equity ratio is very high at 376.02.
Total debt of $48.47B significantly outweighs total cash of $8.83B.
Free cash flow of $2.04B (TTM) is considerably lower than total debt.

news.paragraph: Deere & Company's stock momentum is reportedly waning due to weakness in the agricultural sector and increased fertilizer costs stemming from the Iran war. This comes after the company's last reported quarter showed a significant EPS beat, but overall revenue and earnings growth remain negative year-over-year. While analysts maintain a consensus 'buy' rating with a high average target, the current headwinds suggest a mixed outlook.
